metadata.golden_dawn_title: Root of the Powers of Fire

sections.Correspondences: **Kether** in Atziluth (the Archetypal World). The Ace is the root and seed of the whole suit, the pure power of Fire before it divides. It rules, with its court, one quadrant of the heavens.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: The seed of all fire — pure energy, the spark of creation and will at its origin.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Force misapplied, a beginning without direction.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** Marseille's Ace of Batons is a bare flowering club; Book T names it the **Root of the Powers of Fire** (Aces carry no decan — they are the suit's elemental root in Kether), and RWS gives the hand-from-cloud the budding wand [@web_cunliffe_correspondences; @web_greer_gd_minors]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

The Ace is attributed to Kether (Crown) — the first sephirah, pure unity before differentiation. The Ace of Wands is the root of the powers of Fire: the element in its most concentrated, primordial form, unmediated by any planetary colouring. In GD ritual the Aces correspond to the four Kerubic powers at the corners of the universe.
sections.Scene: A hand emerges from a cloud holding a sprouting wooden club or wand. Small leaves bud from the branch. A castle and landscape appear in the background. The wand points upward.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

A hand issuing from a cloud grasps a stout wand or club. Divinatory Meanings : Creation,
invention, enterprise, the powers which result in these; principle, beginning, source; birth, family,
origin, and in a sense the virility which is behind them; the starting point of enterprises; according
to another account, money, fortune, inheritance. Reversed : Fall, decadence, ruin, perdition, to
perish also a certain clouded joy.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Mars in Aries (the 1st decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Chokmah in Atziluth (the Archetypal World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Bold dominion and influence over others; authority established by will.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Restlessness, a domineering edge, pride overreaching.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

Sephirah: Chokmah (Wisdom) — the second sephirah — pure undifferentiated force, the initial expression of energy before it finds a vessel. In Chokmah the suit element is at its most dynamic and least controlled.

Decan ruler: Mars in Aries — the lord of fiery onset, will encountering its first resistance and emerging victorious.
sections.Scene: A man in a red robe stands on a castle battlement, looking out over sea and land. He holds a globe in his right hand and a wand in his left. A second wand is fixed beside him in the wall. Roses and lilies decorate the parapet.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

A tall man looks from a battlemented roof over sea and shore; he holds a globe in his right hand,
while a staff in his left rests on the battlement; another is fixed in a ring. The Rose and Cross and
Lily should be noticed on the left side. Divinatory Meanings : Between the alternative readings
there is no marriage possible; on the one hand, riches, fortune, magnificence; on the other, physical
suffering, disease, chagrin, sadness, mortification. The design gives one suggestion; here is a lord
overlooking his dominion and alternately contemplating a globe; it looks like the malady, the
mortification, the sadness of Alexander amidst the grandeur of this world's wealth. Reversed :
Surprise, wonder, enchantment, emotion, trouble, fear.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Sun in Aries (the 2nd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Binah in Atziluth (the Archetypal World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Established strength, enterprise realised, pride in work, help from others.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Conceit, arrogance, strength resting on its laurels.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

Sephirah: Binah (Understanding) — the third sephirah — the great organising principle, the womb of form. In Binah the suit energy finds its first structure; what was dynamic becomes shaped.

Decan ruler: Sun in Aries — the solar will confirmed and expanded; the first manifestation of personal authority.
sections.Scene: A figure stands with their back to the viewer on a cliff edge, looking out over water where three ships sail. Three wands are planted in the ground around them. The figure holds one of them lightly with their right hand.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

Three

A calm, stately personage, with his back turned, looking from a cliffs edge at ships passing over
the sea. Three staves are planted in the ground, and he leans slightly on one of them. Divinatory
Meanings : He symbolizes established strength, enterprise, effort, trade, commerce, discovery;
those are his ships, bearing his merchandise, which are sailing over the sea. The card also signifies
able co-operation in business, as if the successful merchant prince were looking from his side
towards yours with a view to help you. Reversed : The end of troubles, suspension or cessation of
adversity, toil and disappointment.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Venus in Aries (the 3rd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Chesed in Atziluth (the Archetypal World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Completion and rest after labour, harmony, the perfected work celebrated.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Work unfinished, a foundation not yet stable.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

Sephirah: Chesed (Mercy) — the fourth sephirah — beneficence, abundance, the consolidation of form. In Chesed the suit energy is stabilised and magnified; Jupiter governs here.

Decan ruler: Venus in Aries — harmony won through force; the peace that follows successful assertion.
sections.Scene: Four wands are planted in the ground, their tops draped with a garland of flowers and fruits. Beyond them, two women celebrate holding bouquets. In the background, the towers and walls of a city are visible.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

Four

From the four great staves planted in the foreground there is a great garland suspended; two female
figures uplift nosegays; at their side is a bridge over a moat, leading to an old manorial house.
Divinatory Meanings : They are for once almost on the surface— country life, haven of refuge, a
species of domestic harvest-home, repose, concord, harmony, prosperity, peace, and the perfected
work of these. Reversed : The meaning remains unaltered; it is prosperity, increase, felicity, beauty,
embellishment.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Saturn in Leo (the 1st decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Geburah in Atziluth (the Archetypal World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Competition, struggle, quarrels — the friction that sharpens.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Pointless contradiction, complications, conflict for its own sake.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

Sephirah: Geburah (Strength) — the fifth sephirah — severity, purifying power, the necessary destruction of what has become too fixed. In Geburah the suit energy is stressed and tested; Mars governs here.

Decan ruler: Saturn in Leo — obstruction to the proud will; the necessary friction that tests the strength of enterprise.
sections.Scene: Five young men engage in a scuffle or mock battle, each brandishing a wand. Their poses are energetic and overlapping. The scene is on a flat plain with no background detail beyond grass.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

Five

A posse of youths, who are brandishing staves, as if in sport or strife. It is mimic warfare, and
hereto correspond the Divinatory Meanings : Imitation, as, for example, sham fight, but also the
strenuous competition and struggle of the search after riches and fortune. In this sense it connects
with the battle of life. Hence some attributions say that it is a card of gold, gain, opulence.
Reversed : Litigation, disputes, trickery, contradiction.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Jupiter in Leo (the 2nd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Tiphareth in Atziluth (the Archetypal World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Victory after strife, success, recognition, good news arriving.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Delay, apprehension, a triumph that breeds complacency.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

Sephirah: Tiphareth (Beauty) — the sixth sephirah — the solar centre, the heart of the tree, the point of perfect balance between above and below. In Tiphareth the suit energy finds its highest expression in the lower tree.

Decan ruler: Jupiter in Leo — the generosity of victorious expansion; Leo's fire blessed by Jupiter's magnanimity.
sections.Scene: A man on horseback carries a wand aloft with a laurel wreath attached to it. Five other figures march beside and below him, each carrying a wand. He appears to be returning in triumph.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

A laurelled horseman bears one staff adorned with a laurel crown; footmen with staves are at his
side. Divinatory Meanings : The card has been so designed that it can cover several significations;
on the surface, it is a victor triumphing, but it is also great news, such as might be carried in state
by the King's courier; it is expectation crowned with its own desire, the crown of hope, and so
forth. Reversed : Apprehension, fear, as of a victorious enemy at the gate; treachery, disloyalty, as
of gates being opened to the enemy; also indefinite delay.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Mars in Leo (the 3rd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Netzach in Atziluth (the Archetypal World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Courage against the odds, holding one's ground from a position of advantage.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Anxiety, yielding ground, valour spent on the wrong fight.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

Sephirah: Netzach (Victory) — the seventh sephirah — desire, instinct, the power of nature unmediated by mind. In Netzach the suit energy enters the sphere of Venus and becomes coloured by longing and creative impulse.

Decan ruler: Mars in Leo — the single will holding against the many; courage as an act of identity.
sections.Scene: A man stands on elevated ground, brandishing a wand at six wands thrust upward toward him from below. His expression is determined. He holds the high ground, defending his position.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

Seven

A young man on a craggy eminence brandishing a staff; six other staves are raised towards him
from below. Divinatory Meanings : It is a card of valour, for, on the surface, six are attacking one,
who has, however, the vantage position. On the intellectual plane, it signifies discussion, wordy
strife; in business— negotiations, war of trade, barter, competition. It is further a card of success, for
the combatant is on the top and his enemies may be unable to reach him. Reversed : Perplexity,
embarrassments, anxiety. It is also a caution against indecision.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Mercury in Sagittarius (the 1st decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Hod in Atziluth (the Archetypal World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Swift action and messages, energy rushing toward its aim.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Haste scattering force, stagnation, arrows loosed too soon.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

Sephirah: Hod (Splendour) — the eighth sephirah — the rational mind, Mercury, the sphere of language and analysis. In Hod the suit energy is broken into multiplicity and re-organised by intellectual operations.

Decan ruler: Mercury in Sagittarius — swift communication of aims; the arrow released with perfect aim.
sections.Scene: Eight wands fly through the air in parallel, tilting slightly downward as if in motion toward their target. The sky is clear. A landscape with a river appears below. There are no human figures.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

Eight

The card represents motion through the immovable-a flight of wands through an open country; but
they draw to the term of their course. That which they signify is at hand; it may be even on the
threshold. Divincitory Meanings: Activity in undertakings, the path of such activity, swiftness, as
that of an express messenger; great haste, great hope, speed towards an end which promises
assured felicity; generally, that which is on the move; also the arrows of love. Reversed: Arrows of
jealousy, internal dispute, stingings of conscience, quarrels; and domestic disputes for persons who
are married.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Moon in Sagittarius (the 2nd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Yesod in Atziluth (the Archetypal World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Strength held in reserve, resilience, recovery, readiness for one more stand.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Obstinacy, exhaustion, defence become rigidity.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

Sephirah: Yesod (Foundation) — the ninth sephirah — the astral plane, the lunar sphere, the collector of all higher influences and their transmitter to the material world. In Yesod the suit energy achieves its pre-material crystallisation.

Decan ruler: Moon in Sagittarius — instinctual resilience; the fire that recovers by returning to its source.
sections.Scene: A bandaged man leans on a wand as if injured or weary. Behind him, eight wands stand in a row like a fence. His expression is guarded but resolute, as if prepared for one more challenge.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

Nine

The figure leans upon his staff and has an expectant look, as if awaiting an enemy. Behind are
eight other staves— erect, in orderly disposition, like a palisade. Divinatory Meanings : The card
signifies strength in opposition. If attacked, the person will meet an onslaught boldly; and his build
shews, that he may prove a formidable antagonist. With this main significance there are all its
possible adjuncts— delay, suspension, adjournment. Reversed : Obstacles, adversity, calamity.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Saturn in Sagittarius (the 3rd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Malkuth in Atziluth (the Archetypal World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Overload, burden, force used cruelly, the weight of too much carried.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: A burden set down, but treachery or collapse may follow.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

Sephirah: Malkuth (Kingdom) — the tenth sephirah — the material world, the earth, the point where all higher forces finally manifest. In Malkuth the suit energy has completed its descent and become physically real.

Decan ruler: Saturn in Sagittarius — the weight that oppresses the fiery ideal with mundane obligation.
sections.Scene: A figure bends under the burden of ten wands, all carried together in both arms, obscuring his face. He walks toward a town visible in the background. His posture expresses effort and encumbrance.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

A man oppressed by the weight of the ten staves which he is carrying. Divinatory Meanings : A
card of many significances, and some of the readings cannot be harmonized. I set aside that which
connects it with honour and good faith. The chief meaning is oppression simply, but it is also
fortune, gain, any kind of success, and then it is the oppression of these things. It is also a card of
false-seeming, disguise, perfidy. The place which the figure is approaching may suffer from the
rods that he carries. Success is stultified if the Nine of Swords follows, and if it is a question of a
lawsuit, there will be certain loss. Reversed : Contrarieties, difficulties, intrigues, and their
analogies.

sections.Golden Dawn Rank: RWS **Knight of Wands** = Golden Dawn **Knight of Wands** — **Fire of Fire**.
sections.Correspondences: Rules 21° Scorpio – 20° Sagittarius. As Fire of Fire, this court is the fire aspect of an ardent and swift suit.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Headlong, active, the mounted warrior who charges and is gone, coloured by the ardent and swift nature of Wands. A person or force of this temperament, or the call to embody it.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The Fire of Fire energy turned excessive, immature, or against itself.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** RWS court names (Page/Knight/Queen/King) differ from **Book T's King/Queen/Prince/Princess**; this rider corresponds to the **King of Wands = Fire of Fire** in the GD scheme [@web_cunliffe_correspondences]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

The Knight (King in RWS) is the fiery part of the suit element — Fire of Fire of Fire. He is the most active expression of the suit: swift, penetrating, potentially consuming. In GD the Knight corresponds to the Yod of the Divine Name, the initial creative spark of the Tetragrammaton as it manifests in the suit's world.
sections.Scene: A knight in red armor charges forward on a leaping horse. He holds a wand at an angle and his scarf streams behind him. The landscape is arid with low hills. The horse rears slightly, full of energy.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

Knight

He is shewn as if upon a journey, armed with a short wand, and although mailed is not on a
warlike errand. He is passing mounds or pyramids. The motion of the horse is a key to the
character of its rider, and suggests the precipitate mood, or things connected therewith. Divinatory
Meanings : Departure, absence, flight, emigration. A dark young man, friendly. Change of
residence. Reversed : Rupture, division, interruption, discord,

sections.Golden Dawn Rank: RWS **Queen of Wands** = Golden Dawn **Queen of Wands** — **Water of Fire**.
sections.Correspondences: Rules 21° Pisces – 20° Aries. As Water of Fire, this court is the water aspect of an ardent and swift suit.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Receptive mastery, magnetic and deep, the power held within, coloured by the ardent and swift nature of Wands. A person or force of this temperament, or the call to embody it.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The Water of Fire energy turned excessive, immature, or against itself.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** Corresponds to **Book T's Queen of Wands (Water of Fire)**; the cat and sunflower are Smith's additions to the Marseille court figure [@web_cunliffe_correspondences]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

The Queen is the watery part of the suit element — Fire of Fire of Water. She is the receptive, internalising expression: her power sustains and nourishes rather than projects. In GD the Queen corresponds to the first Heh of the Tetragrammaton, the great mother principle operating within the suit's domain.
sections.Scene: A queen sits on a golden throne decorated with lion heads and sunflowers. She holds a tall wand in her right hand and a sunflower in her left. A black cat sits at her feet. Sunflowers bloom behind her and lions adorn the throne.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

Queen

The Wands throughout this suit are always in leaf, as it is a suit of life and animation. Emotionally
and otherwise, the Queen's personality corresponds to that of the King, but is more magnetic.
Divinatory Meanings : A dark woman, countrywoman, friendly, chaste, loving, honourable. If the
card beside her signifies a man, she is well disposed towards him; if a woman, she is interested in
the Querent. Also, love of money, or a certain success in business. Reversed : Good, economical,
obliging, serviceable. Signifies also— but in certain positions and in the neighbourhood of other
cards tending in such directions— opposition, jealousy, even deceit and infidelity.

sections.Golden Dawn Rank: RWS **King of Wands** = Golden Dawn **Prince of Wands** — **Air of Fire**.
sections.Correspondences: Rules 21° Cancer – 20° Leo. As Air of Fire, this court is the air aspect of an ardent and swift suit.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: The intellect of the suit in action — swift, reasoning, decisive, coloured by the ardent and swift nature of Wands. A person or force of this temperament, or the call to embody it.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The Air of Fire energy turned excessive, immature, or against itself.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** The RWS "King" maps to the GD **Prince** rank (Air of Fire), a naming offset that runs through all four suits [@web_cunliffe_correspondences]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

The King (Knight in RWS) is the airy part of the suit element — Fire of Fire of Air. He is the swift messenger of the suit: dynamic in transition, transient in any one position. In GD the King corresponds to the Vav of the Tetragrammaton, the son-principle between the two parents.
sections.Scene: A king sits on a throne decorated with lion and salamander motifs. He holds a long wand and wears a robe with salamander emblems. He looks to one side with a forthright expression. A small salamander appears near his feet.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

The physical and emotional nature to which this card is attributed is dark, ardent, lithe, animated,
impassioned, noble. The King uplifts a flowering wand, and wears, like his three correspondences
in the remaining suits, what is called a cap of maintenance beneath his crown. He connects with
the symbol of the lion, which is emblazoned on the back of his throne. Divinatory Meanings : Dark
man, friendly, countryman, generally married, honest and conscientious. The card always signifies
honesty, and may mean news concerning an unexpected heritage to fall in before very long.
Reversed : Good, but severe; austere, yet tolerant.

metadata.rules: one quadrant of the heavens around the North Pole (with the Ace)

sections.Golden Dawn Rank: RWS **Page of Wands** = Golden Dawn **Princess of Wands** — **Earth of Fire**.
sections.Correspondences: Rules one quadrant of the heavens around the North Pole (with the Ace). As Earth of Fire, this court is the earth aspect of an ardent and swift suit.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: The suit made earth — daring, brilliant, the herald and the throne, coloured by the ardent and swift nature of Wands. A person or force of this temperament, or the call to embody it.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The Earth of Fire energy turned excessive, immature, or against itself.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** The RWS "Page" maps to the GD **Princess** rank (Earth of Fire) [@web_cunliffe_correspondences]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Fire / World: Atziluth (Archetypal). Wand = the will of Atziluth, the spark of divine intention before it takes form. Ruled by the Holy Guardian Angel in GD cosmology.

The Page (Princess in GD / Knave in TdM) is the earthy part of the suit element — Fire of Fire of Earth. She is the most material expression of the suit: the crystallised form, the heir, the one who brings the energy down into the physical world. In GD the Page corresponds to the final Heh of the Tetragrammaton, the manifest daughter.
sections.Scene: A young man in a tunic and tall feathered hat stands on a flat plain, holding a tall wand. He looks at the wand intently. Pyramids or mountains appear in the desert background. He stands alone, contemplating.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

WANDS

Page

In a scene similar to the former, a young man stands in the act of proclamation. He is unknown but
faithful, and his tidings are strange. Divinatory Meanings : Dark young man, faithful, a lover, an
envoy, a postman. Beside a man, he will bear favourable testimony concerning him. A dangerous
rival, if followed by the Page of Cups. Has the chief qualities of his suit. He may signify family
intelligence. Reversed : Anecdotes, announcements, evil news. Also indecision and the instability
which accompanies it.

metadata.golden_dawn_title: Root of the Powers of Water

sections.Correspondences: **Kether** in Briah (the Creative World). The Ace is the root and seed of the whole suit, the pure power of Water before it divides. It rules, with its court, one quadrant of the heavens.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: The seed of all water — the wellspring of love, joy, and the fertilising emotions.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Feeling blocked at the source, an empty or overflowing cup.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** Marseille's plain cup becomes the **Root of the Powers of Water**; Smith adds the dove and five streams [@web_cunliffe_correspondences; @web_greer_gd_minors]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

The Ace is attributed to Kether (Crown) — the first sephirah, pure unity before differentiation. The Ace of Cups is the root of the powers of Water: the element in its most concentrated, primordial form, unmediated by any planetary colouring. In GD ritual the Aces correspond to the four Kerubic powers at the corners of the universe.
sections.Scene: A hand emerges from a cloud holding a large golden cup or chalice. Five streams of water pour from the cup into a pool below. A dove descends bearing a wafer inscribed with a cross. Water lilies float below.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

The waters are beneath, and thereon are water-lilies; the hand issues from the cloud, holding in its
palm the cup, from which four streams are pouring; a dove, bearing in its bill a cross-marked Host,
descends to place the Wafer in the Cup; the dew of water is falling on all sides. It is an intimation
of that which may lie behind the Lesser Arcana. Divinatory Meanings : House of the true heart, joy,
content, abode, nourishment, abundance, fertility; Holy Table, felicity hereof. Reversed : House of
the false heart, mutation, instability, revolution.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Venus in Cancer (the 1st decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Chokmah in Briah (the Creative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Love, harmony, union, mutual attraction freely given.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: False love, disunion, a bond cooling.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

Sephirah: Chokmah (Wisdom) — the second sephirah — pure undifferentiated force, the initial expression of energy before it finds a vessel. In Chokmah the suit element is at its most dynamic and least controlled.

Decan ruler: Venus in Cancer — the first meeting of hearts; love at its most tender and unguarded.
sections.Scene: A man and woman face each other, each holding a cup and toasting. Above them, a caduceus with a winged lion floats. Both figures are dressed elaborately, suggesting a betrothal or union.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

A youth and maiden are pledging one another, and above their cups rises the Caduceus of Hermes,
between the great wings of which there appears a lion's head. It is a variant of a sign which is
found in a few old examples of this card. Some curious emblematical meanings are attached to it,
but they do not concern us in this place. Divinatory Meanings : Love, passion, friendship, affinity,
union, concord, sympathy, the interrelation of the sexes, and— as a suggestion apart from all offices
of divination- that desire which is not in Nature, but by which Nature is sanctified.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Mercury in Cancer (the 2nd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Binah in Briah (the Creative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Plenty, joy, celebration, the overflow shared among friends.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Excess, a pleasure that cannot last.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

Sephirah: Binah (Understanding) — the third sephirah — the great organising principle, the womb of form. In Binah the suit energy finds its first structure; what was dynamic becomes shaped.

Decan ruler: Mercury in Cancer — the joy of feeling communicated and celebrated; emotional intelligence in community.
sections.Scene: Three women lift their cups in a celebratory toast, dancing in a circle. Flowers and fruit surround them, with a plentiful harvest visible underfoot.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Three

Maidens in a garden-ground with cups uplifted, as if pledging one another. Divinatory Meanings :
The conclusion of any matter in plenty, perfection and merriment; happy issue, victory, fulfilment,
solace, healing, Reversed : Expedition, dispatch, achievement, end. It signifies also the side of
excess in physical enjoyment, and the pleasures of the senses.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Moon in Cancer (the 3rd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Chesed in Briah (the Creative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Pleasure mixed with weariness, satiety, the apathy of having enough.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: A new feeling stirring, restlessness breaking the spell.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

Sephirah: Chesed (Mercy) — the fourth sephirah — beneficence, abundance, the consolidation of form. In Chesed the suit energy is stabilised and magnified; Jupiter governs here.

Decan ruler: Moon in Cancer — the Moon in its own home; feeling that has become habitual, safe but possibly stagnant.
sections.Scene: A young man sits cross-legged beneath a tree, arms folded, contemplating three cups on the grass before him. A fourth cup is offered by a hand from a cloud, but he does not look at it.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Four

A young man is seated under a tree and contemplates three cups set on the grass before him; an
arm issuing from a cloud offers him another cup. His expression notwithstanding is one of
discontent with his environment. Divinatory Meanings : Weariness, disgust, aversion, imaginary
vexations, as if the wine of this world had caused satiety only; another wine, as if a fairy gift, is
now offered the wastrel, but he sees no consolation therein. This is also a card of blended pleasure.
Reversed : Novelty, presage, new instruction, new relations.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Mars in Scorpio (the 1st decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Geburah in Briah (the Creative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Disappointment within plenty, sorrow, the cup spilled.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Hope returning, what remains gathered up.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

Sephirah: Geburah (Strength) — the fifth sephirah — severity, purifying power, the necessary destruction of what has become too fixed. In Geburah the suit energy is stressed and tested; Mars governs here.

Decan ruler: Mars in Scorpio — the wound inflicted by Scorpio's passion; loss as the cost of deep feeling.
sections.Scene: A cloaked figure faces three spilled cups on the ground, their contents flowing away. Behind the figure, two cups remain upright. A bridge and castle appear in the background.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Five

A dark, cloaked figure, looking sideways at three prone cups two others stand upright behind him;
a bridge is in the background, leading to a small keep or holding. Divanatory Meanings: It is a
card of loss, but something remains over; three have been taken, but two are left; it is a card of
inheritance, patrimony, transmission, but not corresponding to expectations; with some interpreters
it is a card of marriage, but not without bitterness or frustration. Reversed: News, alliances,
affinity, consanguinity, ancestry, return, false projects.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Sun in Scorpio (the 2nd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Tiphareth in Briah (the Creative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Well-being, harmony, the sweet pleasure of memory and the past.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Living in the past, nostalgia that holds one back.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

Sephirah: Tiphareth (Beauty) — the sixth sephirah — the solar centre, the heart of the tree, the point of perfect balance between above and below. In Tiphareth the suit energy finds its highest expression in the lower tree.

Decan ruler: Sun in Scorpio — the light within the dark waters; joy recovered after depth.
sections.Scene: A young boy hands a cup filled with white flowers to a young girl in a garden. Five more cups similarly planted with flowers stand around them. A figure in a cloak walks away in the background.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Children in an old garden, their cups filled with flowers. Divinatory Meanings : A card of the past
and of memories, looking back, as— for example— on childhood; happiness, enjoyment, but coming
rather from the past; things that have vanished. Another reading reverses this, giving new relations,
new knowledge, new environment, and then the children are disporting in an unfamiliar precinct.
Reversed l: The future, renewal, that which will come to pass presently.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Venus in Scorpio (the 3rd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Netzach in Briah (the Creative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Illusion, fantasy, deceptive success, intoxication with one's own visions.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Clarity returning, will reasserted over fantasy.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

Sephirah: Netzach (Victory) — the seventh sephirah — desire, instinct, the power of nature unmediated by mind. In Netzach the suit energy enters the sphere of Venus and becomes coloured by longing and creative impulse.

Decan ruler: Venus in Scorpio — desire dissolved into fantasy; beauty distorted by Scorpio's intensity into illusion.
sections.Scene: A silhouetted figure reaches toward seven cups floating in a cloud, each containing a different vision: a castle, gems, a wreath, a dragon, a human face, a veiled figure, and a serpent.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Seven

Strange chalices of vision, but the images are more especially those of the fantastic spirit.
Divinatory Meanings : Fairy favours, images of reflection, sentiment, imagination, things seen in
the glass of contemplation; some attainment in these degrees, but nothing permanent or substantial
is suggested. Reversed : Desire, will, determination, project.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Saturn in Pisces (the 1st decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Hod in Briah (the Creative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Leaving behind what was won to seek a deeper meaning; withdrawal.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Aimless drifting, abandonment without purpose.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

Sephirah: Hod (Splendour) — the eighth sephirah — the rational mind, Mercury, the sphere of language and analysis. In Hod the suit energy is broken into multiplicity and re-organised by intellectual operations.

Decan ruler: Saturn in Pisces — the withdrawal from feeling into solitude; Saturnine contraction in the most diffuse water sign.
sections.Scene: A figure in a red cloak walks away through a mountainous night landscape, leaving behind eight upright cups arranged in a row. The moon is visible in the upper sky. The cups stand abandoned.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Eight

A man of dejected aspect is deserting the cups of his felicity, enterprise, undertaking or previous
concern. Divinatory Meanings : The card speaks for itself on the surface, but other readings are
entirely antithetical— giving joy, mildness, timidity, honour, modesty. In practice, it is usually
found that the card shews the decline of a matter, or that a matter which has been thought to be
important is really of slight consequence— either for good or evil. Reversed : Great joy, happiness,
feasting.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Jupiter in Pisces (the 2nd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Yesod in Briah (the Creative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: The 'wish' card — contentment, physical well-being, fulfilment.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Smugness, excess, satisfaction grown complacent.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

Sephirah: Yesod (Foundation) — the ninth sephirah — the astral plane, the lunar sphere, the collector of all higher influences and their transmitter to the material world. In Yesod the suit energy achieves its pre-material crystallisation.

Decan ruler: Jupiter in Pisces — abundance of feeling; the happiness of one who has surrendered to the flow.
sections.Scene: A satisfied man sits with arms folded before a curved arrangement of nine golden cups on a shelf behind him. His posture suggests contentment and material well-being.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Nine

A goodly personage has feasted to his heart's content, and abundant refreshment of wine is on the
arched counter behind him, seeming to indicate that the future is also assured. The picture offers
the material side only, but there are other aspects. Divinatory Meanings : Concord, contentment,
physical bien-etre\ also victory, success, advantage; satisfaction for the Querent or person for
whom the consultation is made. Reversed : Truth, loyalty, liberty; but the readings vary and include
mistakes, imperfections, etc.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Mars in Pisces (the 3rd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Malkuth in Briah (the Creative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Lasting happiness, harmony, the suit fulfilled in shared joy.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Discord beneath a calm surface, a peace not quite real.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

Sephirah: Malkuth (Kingdom) — the tenth sephirah — the material world, the earth, the point where all higher forces finally manifest. In Malkuth the suit energy has completed its descent and become physically real.

Decan ruler: Mars in Pisces — the end of an emotional cycle; force spent, family achieved through yielding.
sections.Scene: A couple stands arm in arm beneath a rainbow arc of ten cups. Two children dance nearby. A house and green hills form a peaceful background. The scene evokes domestic fulfillment.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Appearance of Cups in a rainbow; it is contemplated in wonder and ecstacy by a man and woman
below, evidently husband and wife. His right arm is about her; his left is raised upward; she raises
her right arm. The two children dancing near them have not observed the prodigy but are happy
after their own manner. There is a home-scene beyond. Divinatory Meanings : Contentment, repose
of the entire heart; the perfection of that state; also perfection of human love and friendship; if with
several picture-cards, a person who is taking charge of the Querent's interests; also the town,
village or country inhabited by the Querent. Reversed : Repose of the false heart, indignation,
violence.

sections.Golden Dawn Rank: RWS **Knight of Cups** = Golden Dawn **Knight of Cups** — **Fire of Water**.
sections.Correspondences: Rules 21° Aquarius – 20° Pisces. As Fire of Water, this court is the fire aspect of a tender and dreaming suit.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Headlong, active, the mounted warrior who charges and is gone, coloured by the tender and dreaming nature of Cups. A person or force of this temperament, or the call to embody it.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The Fire of Water energy turned excessive, immature, or against itself.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** RWS "Knight" maps to GD **King** rank (Fire of Water) [@web_cunliffe_correspondences]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

The Knight (King in RWS) is the fiery part of the suit element — Water of Water of Fire. He is the most active expression of the suit: swift, penetrating, potentially consuming. In GD the Knight corresponds to the Yod of the Divine Name, the initial creative spark of the Tetragrammaton as it manifests in the suit's world.
sections.Scene: A knight in armor rides slowly on a calm gray horse across flat ground near water. He holds a cup forward in his right hand and looks into it. His helmet sports large wings. His movement is deliberate and unhurried.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Knight

Graceful, but not warlike; riding quietly, wearing a winged helmet, referring to those higher graces
of the imagination which sometimes characterize this card. He too is a dreamer, but the images of
the side of sense haunt him in his vision. Divinatory Meanings : Arrival, approach— sometimes that
of a messenger; advances, proposition, demeanour, invitation, incitement. Reversed : Trickery,
artifice, subtlety, swindling, duplicity, fraud.

sections.Golden Dawn Rank: RWS **Queen of Cups** = Golden Dawn **Queen of Cups** — **Water of Water**.
sections.Correspondences: Rules 21° Gemini – 20° Cancer. As Water of Water, this court is the water aspect of a tender and dreaming suit.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Receptive mastery, magnetic and deep, the power held within, coloured by the tender and dreaming nature of Cups. A person or force of this temperament, or the call to embody it.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The Water of Water energy turned excessive, immature, or against itself.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** Corresponds to **Queen of Cups (Water of Water)**; the elaborate chalice is Smith's [@web_cunliffe_correspondences]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

The Queen is the watery part of the suit element — Water of Water of Water. She is the receptive, internalising expression: her power sustains and nourishes rather than projects. In GD the Queen corresponds to the first Heh of the Tetragrammaton, the great mother principle operating within the suit's domain.
sections.Scene: A queen sits on a throne at the edge of the sea. She gazes into an elaborate covered cup, as if reading it. Her throne is decorated with fish and mermaids. She is contemplative and absorbed.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Queen

Beautiful, fair, dreamy— as one who sees visions in a cup. This is, however, only one of her
aspects; she sees, but she also acts, and her activity feeds her dream. Divinatory Meanings : Good,
fair woman; honest, devoted woman, who will do service to the Querent; loving intelligence, and
hence the gift of vision; success, happiness, pleasure; also wisdom, virtue; a perfect spouse and a
good mother. Reversed : The accounts vary; good woman; otherwise, distinguished woman but one
not to be trusted; perverse woman; vice, dishonour, depravity.

sections.Golden Dawn Rank: RWS **King of Cups** = Golden Dawn **Prince of Cups** — **Air of Water**.
sections.Correspondences: Rules 21° Libra – 20° Scorpio. As Air of Water, this court is the air aspect of a tender and dreaming suit.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: The intellect of the suit in action — swift, reasoning, decisive, coloured by the tender and dreaming nature of Cups. A person or force of this temperament, or the call to embody it.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The Air of Water energy turned excessive, immature, or against itself.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** RWS "King" maps to GD **Prince** rank (Air of Water) [@web_cunliffe_correspondences]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Water / World: Briah (Creative). Cup = the creative imagination of Briah, the vessel in which the divine intention takes feeling and form. Ruled by the Archangelic world.

The King (Knight in RWS) is the airy part of the suit element — Water of Water of Air. He is the swift messenger of the suit: dynamic in transition, transient in any one position. In GD the King corresponds to the Vav of the Tetragrammaton, the son-principle between the two parents.
sections.Scene: A king sits on a throne floating amid rough waters. He holds a cup in one hand and a scepter in the other. A fish leaps from the sea behind him. Despite the tumult of the water, he sits in command.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

King

He holds a short sceptre in his left hand and a great cup in his right; his throne is set upon the sea;
on one side a ship is riding and on the other a dolphin is leaping. The implicit is that the Sign of
the Cup naturally refers to water, which appears in all the court cards. Divinatory Meanings : Fair
man, man of business, law, or divinity; responsible, disposed to oblige the Querent; also equity, art
and science, including those who profess science, law and art; creative intelligence. Reversed :
Dishonest, double-dealing man; roguery, exaction, injustice, vice, scandal, pillage, considerable
loss.

sections.Scene: A young man in a flower-patterned tunic stands at the sea's edge, looking at a fish that has emerged from the cup he holds. The fish appears to speak to him. He holds the cup gingerly, surprised.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

CUPS

Page

PACE dj CUP 5,

A fair, pleasing, somewhat effeminate page, of studious and intent aspect, contemplates a fish
rising from a cup to look at him. It is the pictures of the mind taking form. Divinatory Meanings :
Fair young man, one impelled to render service and with whom the Querent will be connected; a
studious youth; news, message; application, reflection, meditation; also these things directed to
business. Reversed : Taste, inclination, attachment, seduction, deception, artifice.

metadata.golden_dawn_title: Root of the Powers of Air

sections.Correspondences: **Kether** in Yetzirah (the Formative World). The Ace is the root and seed of the whole suit, the pure power of Air before it divides. It rules, with its court, one quadrant of the heavens.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: The seed of all air — the sword of mind and truth, force invoked for good or ill.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The double-edged blade turned the wrong way; reason used to wound.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** Marseille's plain sword becomes the **Root of the Powers of Air**; Smith crowns the blade [@web_cunliffe_correspondences; @web_greer_gd_minors]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].

*Full card-by-card research:* `research/cards/golden-dawn-book-t-tarot.md`.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

The Ace is attributed to Kether (Crown) — the first sephirah, pure unity before differentiation. The Ace of Swords is the root of the powers of Air: the element in its most concentrated, primordial form, unmediated by any planetary colouring. In GD ritual the Aces correspond to the four Kerubic powers at the corners of the universe.
sections.Scene: A hand emerges from a cloud holding an upright sword. A golden crown sits on the tip of the blade, hung with a wreath. Mountains and a valley appear below. The crown suggests victory or judgment.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

A hand issues from a cloud, grasping as word, the point of which is encircled by a crown.
Divinatory Meanings : Triumph, the excessive degree in everything, conquest, triumph of force. It
is a card of great force, in love as well as in hatred. The crown may carry a much higher
significance than comes usually within the sphere of fortune-telling. Reversed : The same, but the
results are disastrous; another account says— conception, childbirth, augmentation, multiplicity.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Moon in Libra (the 1st decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Chokmah in Yetzirah (the Formative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Truce, a balance held by tension, peace restored after conflict.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Duplicity, the stalemate broken the wrong way.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

Sephirah: Chokmah (Wisdom) — the second sephirah — pure undifferentiated force, the initial expression of energy before it finds a vessel. In Chokmah the suit element is at its most dynamic and least controlled.

Decan ruler: Moon in Libra — the truce of feeling; thoughts balanced by the desire for peace rather than resolved.
sections.Scene: A blindfolded woman sits before the sea, holding two crossed swords horizontally across her chest. Behind her the water is calm and a crescent moon is reflected in it. Her posture is still, balanced, and defended.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

A hoodwinked female figure balances two swords upon her shoulders. Divinatory Meanings :
Conformity and the equipoise which it suggests, courage, friendship, concord in a state of arms;
another reading gives tenderness, affection, intimacy. The suggestion of harmony and other
favourable readings must be considered in a qualified manner, as Swords generally are not
symbolical of beneficent forces in human affairs. Reversed : Imposture, falsehood, duplicity,
disloyalty.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Saturn in Libra (the 2nd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Binah in Yetzirah (the Formative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Heartbreak, grief, separation — sorrow that clears the air.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Pain that lingers, a slow and grudging recovery.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

Sephirah: Binah (Understanding) — the third sephirah — the great organising principle, the womb of form. In Binah the suit energy finds its first structure; what was dynamic becomes shaped.

Decan ruler: Saturn in Libra — sorrow crystallised by Saturnine clarity; the pain of seeing the truth of a relationship.
sections.Scene: Three swords pierce a red heart, which floats against a gray sky with rain falling. There are no figures — only the heart and the swords.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

Three

Three swords piercing a heart; cloud and rain behind. Divinatory Meanings : Removal, absence,
delay, division, rupture, dispersion, and all that the design signifies naturally, being too simple and
obvious to call for specific enumeration. Reversed : Mental alienation, error, loss, distraction,
disorder, confusion.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Jupiter in Libra (the 3rd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Chesed in Yetzirah (the Formative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Repose, recovery, retreat, the rest that heals.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Restlessness, a return to the fray too soon.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

Sephirah: Chesed (Mercy) — the fourth sephirah — beneficence, abundance, the consolidation of form. In Chesed the suit energy is stabilised and magnified; Jupiter governs here.

Decan ruler: Jupiter in Libra — the mercy of rest; Jovian recuperation from the sharpness of conflict.
sections.Scene: A knight lies in effigy on a stone tomb, hands clasped in prayer. Three swords hang on the wall above him; a fourth lies along his side. A stained-glass window shows a figure kneeling before another.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

Four

The effigy of a knight in the attitude of prayer, at full length upon his tomb. Divinatory Meanings:
Vigilance, retreat, solitude, hermit's repose, exile, tomb and coffin. It is these last that have
suggested the design. Reversed: Wise administration, circumspection, economy, avarice,
precaution, testament.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Venus in Aquarius (the 1st decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Geburah in Yetzirah (the Formative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Defeat, loss, dishonour, the costs of winning at any price.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: An empty victory, resentment that outlives the fight.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

Sephirah: Geburah (Strength) — the fifth sephirah — severity, purifying power, the necessary destruction of what has become too fixed. In Geburah the suit energy is stressed and tested; Mars governs here.

Decan ruler: Venus in Aquarius — defeat through cunning; the hollow victory of a mind without compassion.
sections.Scene: A smirking man collects three swords while two defeated figures walk away in the distance, their swords left on the ground. The sky is cloudy and turbulent. The victor seems satisfied; the losers are dejected.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

Five

A disdainful man looks after two retreating and dejected figures. Their swords lie upon the ground.
He carries two others on his left shoulder, and a third sword is in his right hand, point to earth. He
is the master in possession of the field. Divinatory Meanings : Degradation, destruction, revocation,
infamy, dishonour, loss, with the variants and analogues of these. Reversed : The same; burial and
obsequies.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Mercury in Aquarius (the 2nd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Tiphareth in Yetzirah (the Formative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Passage from trouble toward calmer water, progress earned.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: A transition stalled, the far shore not yet reached.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

Sephirah: Tiphareth (Beauty) — the sixth sephirah — the solar centre, the heart of the tree, the point of perfect balance between above and below. In Tiphareth the suit energy finds its highest expression in the lower tree.

Decan ruler: Mercury in Aquarius — the rational faculty guiding passage; thought that finds the channel through difficulty.
sections.Scene: A ferryman poles a boat across water, carrying two passengers: a woman with a child, huddled beneath a cloak. Six swords stand upright in the prow of the boat. They move from rough water toward calmer water.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

A ferryman carrying passengers in his punt to the further shore. The course is smooth, and seeing
that the freight is light, it may be noted that the work is not beyond his strength. Divinatory
Meanings : journey by water, route, way, envoy, commissionary, expedient. Reversed : Declaration,
confession, publicity; one account says that it is a proposal of love.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Moon in Aquarius (the 3rd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Netzach in Yetzirah (the Formative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Cunning, a partial and unreliable success, slipping away with the prize.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Exposure, the plan undone, what was taken returned.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

Sephirah: Netzach (Victory) — the seventh sephirah — desire, instinct, the power of nature unmediated by mind. In Netzach the suit energy enters the sphere of Venus and becomes coloured by longing and creative impulse.

Decan ruler: Moon in Aquarius — the unstable plan, the half-commitment; feeling masquerading as strategy.
sections.Scene: A man tiptoes away from a camp, carrying five swords. He looks back over his shoulder with a sly expression. Two swords remain planted in the ground behind him. Tents and figures in the distance.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

Seven

A man in the act of carrying away five swords rapidly; the two others of the card remain stuck in
the ground. A camp is close at hand. Divinatory Meanings : Design, attempt, wish, hope,
confidence; also quarrelling, a plan that may fail, annoyance. The design is uncertain in its import,
because the significations are widely at variance with each other. Reversed : Good advice, counsel,
instruction, slander, babbling.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Jupiter in Gemini (the 1st decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Hod in Yetzirah (the Formative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Restriction, constraint, force turned against itself, indecision.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Release, the bonds loosened, freedom found within limits.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

Sephirah: Hod (Splendour) — the eighth sephirah — the rational mind, Mercury, the sphere of language and analysis. In Hod the suit energy is broken into multiplicity and re-organised by intellectual operations.

Decan ruler: Jupiter in Gemini — the interference of multiplying thoughts; the trap built from the mind's own generativity.
sections.Scene: A woman stands bound and blindfolded, surrounded by eight swords planted in the ground around her in a loose circle. She stands in a marsh near water. A castle appears in the background. She is trapped but the swords do not touch her.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

Eight

A woman, bound and hoodwinked, with the swords of the card about her. Y et it is rather a card of
temporary durance than of irretrievable bondage. Divinatory Meanings : Bad news, violent chagrin,
crisis, censure, power in trammels, conflict, calumny; also sickness. Reversed : Disquiet, difficulty,
opposition, accident, treachery; what is unforeseen; fatality.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Mars in Gemini (the 2nd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Yesod in Yetzirah (the Formative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Anguish, nightmare, cruelty, the long night of despair.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The worst passing, a first grey light of dawn.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

Sephirah: Yesod (Foundation) — the ninth sephirah — the astral plane, the lunar sphere, the collector of all higher influences and their transmitter to the material world. In Yesod the suit energy achieves its pre-material crystallisation.

Decan ruler: Mars in Gemini — the twin anxieties of mind and desire; the nightmare generated by the war-god in the sign of duality.
sections.Scene: A figure sits up in bed, face buried in hands, in an attitude of despair. Nine swords hang horizontally on the wall behind them, all parallel. A patterned quilt covers the lower half of the figure. Night surrounds them.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

Nine

One seated on her couch in lamentation, with the swords over her. She is as one who knows no
sorrow which is like unto hers. It is a card of utter desolation. Divinatory Meanings : Death, failure,
miscarriage, delay, deception, disappointment, despair. Reversed : Imprisonment, suspicion, doubt,
reasonable fear, shame.

sections.Correspondences:
**Decan:** Sun in Gemini (the 3rd decanate of its sign).

**Sephirah:** Malkuth in Yetzirah (the Formative World).
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Ruin, the end of an illusion, the failure of force, rock bottom.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: Recovery — from here the only way is up.
sections.Symbol:
Suit element: Air / World: Yetzirah (Formative). Sword = the formative mind of Yetzirah, the plane of astral patterns, thought-forms, and angelic intelligences.

Sephirah: Malkuth (Kingdom) — the tenth sephirah — the material world, the earth, the point where all higher forces finally manifest. In Malkuth the suit energy has completed its descent and become physically real.

Decan ruler: Sun in Gemini — the culmination of mental suffering; the ruin of every partial strategy as the solar light exposes them all simultaneously.
sections.Scene: A figure lies face down on the ground with ten swords plunged into their back. The sky above is dark and stormy on the left but brightening on the horizon at right. The figure is still; the scene is one of complete defeat.
sections.Waite:
[A. E. Waite, *The Pictorial Key to the Tarot*, 1911 — a later esoteric reading describing the Rider–Waite–Smith imagery.]

SWORDS

A prostrate figure, pierced by all the swords belonging to the card. Divinatory Meanings :
Whatsoever is intimated by the design; also pain, affliction, tears, sadness, desolation. It is not
especially a card of violent death. Reversed : Advantage, profit, success, favour, but none of these
are permanent; also power and authority.

sections.Golden Dawn Rank: RWS **Knight of Swords** = Golden Dawn **Knight of Swords** — **Fire of Air**.
sections.Correspondences: Rules 21° Taurus – 20° Gemini. As Fire of Air, this court is the fire aspect of a keen and restless suit.
sections.Divinatory Meaning: Headlong, active, the mounted warrior who charges and is gone, coloured by the keen and restless nature of Swords. A person or force of this temperament, or the call to embody it.
sections.Reversed / Ill-Dignified: The Fire of Air energy turned excessive, immature, or against itself.
sections.Research note:
**What changed (vs. its parent):** RWS "Knight" maps to GD **King** rank (Fire of Air) [@web_cunliffe_correspondences]. 

*Sources:* [@waite1910], [@web_cunliffe_correspondences].
